title: What is DarkForest.fm?
body: DarkForest.fm is an independent online radio station exploring music, stories, art, mystery, and the unknown.

title: How can I listen?
body: You can listen directly through the website. Click the "Play" button above.

title: Who runs DarkForest.fm?
body:
For now, Dark Forest FM is simply one person following an idea into the woods and seeing where the path leads.

If you’d like to know more about the project and the person behind it, you’ll find the story on the About page.

title: Where does the music come from?
body: The music played on DarkForest.fm is either Creative Commons licensed or shared through a direct agreement between the artist and the radio. No commercially licensed music is played on the station.

title: Can I submit music?
body: Yes. We are always interested in discovering new music and independent artists. Use the contact form or write to us directly.

title: Can I suggest a topic, story, or idea?
body: Absolutely. Recommendations, strange discoveries, interesting links, forgotten histories, and unexpected inspirations are all welcome.

title: Is this project vibe-coded?
body:
No.

Dark Forest FM is largely hand-crafted, including the design, infrastructure, and most of the code. Modern AI tools occasionally assist with writing and editing, but the project itself is built intentionally, one piece at a time.

title: What is the long-term vision?
body: To create a place worth returning to: a broadcast for night owls, travelers, dreamers, builders, artists, and anyone drawn to the edge of the map.

title: Why are you asking for donations?
body: Running an online radio station requires servers, bandwidth, storage, and ongoing maintenance. Donations help keep the broadcast alive and allow the project to grow.
